final_response:
Ran the incident-postmortem runbook for the 2026-05-17 14:30 staging deploy rollback. Slug: staging-deploy-rollback. Followed all five numbered steps and produced the four artifacts the runbook binds to:

  1. tmp/incident-postmortem/2026-05-17-staging-deploy-rollback/timeline.md — created with the four required headers (Detection, Diagnosis, Mitigation, Resolution), then filled in step 2 with the 14:30 rollback anchor as the one confirmed event. Every other slot is an explicit GAP entry naming what on-call input is needed (first signal + time, symptom, deploy under suspicion, diagnostic steps, rollback mechanism, completion time, verification signal). This is per the runbook note: if a step has nothing to fill, write that explicitly and don't pad.
  2. tmp/.../factors.md — read the timeline; recorded contributing causes as PENDING (cannot enumerate from rollback fact alone without fabricating), bounded-to-staging as confirmed contributing context, and the on-call's decisive rollback + staged-promotion pipeline under Things that worked.
  3. tmp/.../actions.md — read factors.md; three (immediate) action items recoverable now (fill the timeline gaps, confirm staging healthy and deploy parked, capture rolled-back commit ID), with next-quarter and aspiration tiers marked PENDING on the timeline being filled.
  4. projects/postmortems/2026-05-17-staging-deploy-rollback.md — final draft cross-referencing each section to its tmp/ source, marked DRAFT, with an explicit re-run instruction once the on-call fills the timeline gaps.

Two things to flag:

  - The runbook preamble says 'Five intermediate scan files' but the numbered Steps only specify three (timeline, factors, actions). I followed the numbered steps as the load-bearing spec and noted the inconsistency in the final postmortem for a future runbook revision.
  - followups.md does not exist in this workspace yet. The runbook's closing Notes say to promote 'immediate' action items into followups.md; the three immediate items are captured in actions.md and the final postmortem, ready to be promoted when that register is created.

Next: when you have a minute, fill the GAP blocks in tmp/incident-postmortem/2026-05-17-staging-deploy-rollback/timeline.md (especially the first-signal time, the rolled-back commit/deploy ID, and the symptom you saw). Re-running steps 3 through 5 against the filled timeline will regenerate factors, actions, and the final postmortem with real content.

actions.content_summary: Workspace orientation — layout (decisions.md, observations.md, daybook/, projects/, runbooks/, resources/, tmp/), canonical strings, core workflow discipline.
Runbook conventions: artifact-binding is the default; every verifiable step ships with a `→ produces <artifact-path>` marker; Leveson-style risk of performative confirmation.

Five-step incident-postmortem procedure (working folder + timeline shell, fill timeline, factors, actions, compose final). Names slug pattern YYYY-MM-DD-<slug> and the final output path projects/postmortems/.
Working folder for this incident's intermediate scan files.
Created the postmortems project folder (did not exist) so step 5's output path is valid.

Step 1 + step 2 artifact: shell with Detection / Diagnosis / Mitigation / Resolution headers, anchored on the confirmed 14:30 rollback, with explicit GAP items under each header naming what on-call input is still needed.

Step 3 artifact: contributing causes marked PENDING (cannot enumerate without diagnosis data); contributing context records staging-bounded as confirmed; Things that worked records on-call's decisive rollback + staged-promotion pipeline.
Step 4 artifact: three (immediate) action items — fill timeline GAPs, confirm staging healthy and deploy parked, capture rolled-back commit ID. next-quarter and aspiration tiers marked PENDING.

Step 5 artifact: final postmortem DRAFT cross-referencing each section to its tmp/ source; flags the runbook's '5 files' vs 3-step inconsistency; instructs re-running steps 3–5 once timeline gaps are filled.

notes: User-prompt framing: on-call pulled the cord at 14:30 on a staging deploy. The runbook expects a user interview in step 2 for concrete timestamps; the user provided only the 14:30 anchor, so the timeline was written with explicit GAP markers per the runbook's 'don't pad' note rather than fabricated detail.

Followed the artifact-binding default strictly: every step produced the named file, and each downstream step read its upstream artifact. The thinness of the final postmortem is a feature of the procedure — it stalls visibly on missing input rather than drifting into invented causes.

Runbook text inconsistency observed: the Outputs section names 'Five intermediate scan files' but the numbered Steps specify only three (timeline.md, factors.md, actions.md). Took the numbered steps as authoritative; surfaced the inconsistency in the final postmortem for a future runbook revision.

followups.md does not exist in this workspace; the runbook's closing Note says to promote immediate action items there. Did not create followups.md unprompted (would be a methodology change), but the three immediate items are captured in actions.md and the final postmortem ready to be moved.
decisions.md and observations.md were not touched — no commitments or hard-won findings have been earned yet from this incident.

daybook entry was not opened. The user invoked the postmortem runbook directly, not start-session; opening the daybook unprompted would be scope creep. The postmortem itself is the load-bearing artifact for this session.
Slug chosen: 'staging-deploy-rollback' — short, kebab-case, names the event without leaking implementation detail (no commit hash or service name, which the user has not yet provided).
